name: Local 804
description_short: Teamsters Local 804 is a labor union providing member advocacy, contract negotiation, and workplace rights education for UPS and other delivery workers in New York City.
description_long: Teamsters Local 804 is a union local representing workers in the transportation and delivery sectors, primarily UPS employees in the New York City area. The organization focuses on collective bargaining, workplace safety advocacy, and member education rather than financial services. Their primary mission centers on protecting union jobs, negotiating contracts, and ensuring fair labor standards for drivers and warehouse workers. The union publishes research on workplace hazards, particularly around e-commerce and last-mile delivery safety, and actively engages in legislative advocacy for worker protections. Members receive access to union contracts, steward training resources, and detailed information about their workplace rights. Local 804 distinguishes itself through aggressive advocacy against gig economy competition and corporate consolidation, documented in member communications and published reports. The organization provides genuine, non-profit member support services funded through union dues rather than commercial financial products. However, membership is employment-based and limited to union-represented workers, and the organization does not provide consumer finance services, making the 'payday-alternatives' categorization fundamentally incorrect.
services: Union contract negotiation and enforcement for UPS and affiliated employers
Member handbook and workplace rights education materials
Weingarten Rights coaching for union representation during management investigations
OJS (on-job supervision) protection questionnaire and member guidance
9.5 list registration and daily hour limit enforcement with grievance filing
Telehealth benefits coordination (LiveHealthOnline for full-timers, Teladoc for part-timers)
Health & Welfare Fund and COBRA premium management for retirees
Clerk seniority list access and verification
Retirement planning seminars and financial education
Legislative advocacy and workplace safety reporting
New York State Paid Family Leave form assistance and guidance
pros: Free access to full union contracts and negotiated agreements for members
Comprehensive member rights education including Weingarten Rights and OJS protections
Active legislative advocacy resulting in concrete policy recommendations (e.g., Delivery Protection Act)
Telehealth benefits access for full-time employees and retirees through established providers
Detailed steward training resources and member handbooks to enforce workplace rights
9.5 list protections ensuring limits on daily work hours with escalating pay penalties for violations
Published research on workplace safety hazards in e-commerce and last-mile delivery
cons: Membership limited to employed union workers; no services available to general public or non-union members
Does not provide consumer finance products, emergency loans, or credit alternatives
Services focused on workplace advocacy rather than personal financial wellness or credit building
Misclassified as 'payday-alternatives' despite offering zero financial lending or credit products
best_for: UPS drivers and warehouse workers in New York City seeking union representation and contract enforcement
Teamsters Local 804 members needing workplace rights education and steward resources
Workers advocating for e-commerce delivery safety standards and labor protections
diagnosis: Teamsters Local 804 is a labor union providing legitimate member advocacy and workplace rights support, not a consumer finance provider. This organization should not be categorized as 'payday-alternatives' or any financial services category—it belongs in 'free-help' as a non-profit member organization providing educational and advocacy services. Consumers searching for financial assistance products will find no relevant services here.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
